title: Xenial Slow Uruguayan Beef Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful Uruguayan stew made with tender beef, peppercorns, and a rich wine broth, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This macrobiotic-friendly dish is cooked to perfection using sous-vide techniques.
instructions: Preheat the sous-vide machine to 130 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the beef cubes with salt and black peppercorns.
Heat the red wine and beef broth in a saucepan over medium heat until reduced by half.
Add the garlic, onion, carrots, and potatoes to the saucepan and cook until the vegetables are tender.
Seal the beef cubes in a sous-vide bag with the reduced wine broth and cook for 24 hours.
Remove the beef from the bag and slice into thin strips.
Strain the cooking liquid and discard the solids.
Serve the beef strips with the strained cooking liquid spooned over the top.
Garnish with fresh herbs, if desired.
